```{r, echo=FALSE, results="hide"}
repeat {
  a <- sample(2:5,1); pneg <- sample(seq(0.15,0.45,0.05),1)
  pzero <- sample(seq(0.10,0.30,0.05),1); ppos <- 1-pneg-pzero
  ans <- pneg+ppos
  vals <- round(c(ans,ppos,pneg,pzero,ppos-pneg),3)
  if (length(unique(vals)) == 5L) break
}
opts <- sprintf("$%.3f$",vals)
```

Question
========
Uma variĂˇvel $X$ assume $-`r a`, $0$ e $`r a` com probabilidades `r pneg`, `r pzero` e `r sprintf("%.2f",ppos)`. Para $Y=X^2$, qual Ă© $P(Y=`r a^2`)$?

Answerlist
----------
* `r opts[1]`
* `r opts[2]`
* `r opts[3]`
* `r opts[4]`
* `r opts[5]`

Solution
========
Os valores $X=-`r a`$ e $X=`r a`$ produzem o mesmo valor $Y=`r a^2`$. Portanto, somam-se as massas:
$$P(Y=`r a^2`)=`r pneg`+`r sprintf("%.2f",ppos)`
=`r sprintf("%.3f",ans)`.$$

Meta-information
================
exname: Cap1S6Q5_transformacao_quadrado_discreta
extype: schoice
exsolution: 10000
exshuffle: TRUE
